client/src/components/ui/progress.tsx.content:
"use client"

import * as React from "react"
import * as ProgressPrimitive from "@radix-ui/react-progress"

import { cn } from "@/lib/utils"

const Progress = React.forwardRef<
  React.ElementRef<typeof ProgressPrimitive.Root>,
  React.ComponentPropsWithoutRef<typeof ProgressPrimitive.Root>
>(({ className, value, ...props }, ref) => (
  <ProgressPrimitive.Root
    ref={ref}
    className={cn(
      "relative h-4 w-full overflow-hidden rounded-full bg-secondary",
      className
    )}
    {...props}
  >
    <ProgressPrimitive.Indicator
      className="h-full w-full flex-1 bg-primary transition-all"
      style={{ transform: `translateX(-${100 - (value || 0)}%)` }}
    />
  </ProgressPrimitive.Root>
))
Progress.displayName = ProgressPrimitive.Root.displayName

export { Progress }

import * as React from "react"
import * as SliderPrimitive from "@radix-ui/react-slider"

import { cn } from "@/lib/utils"

const Slider = React.forwardRef<
  React.ElementRef<typeof SliderPrimitive.Root>,
  React.ComponentPropsWithoutRef<typeof SliderPrimitive.Root>
>(({ className, ...props }, ref) => (
  <SliderPrimitive.Root
    ref={ref}
    className={cn(
      "relative flex w-full touch-none select-none items-center",
      className
    )}
    {...props}
  >
    <SliderPrimitive.Track className="relative h-2 w-full grow overflow-hidden rounded-full bg-secondary">
      <SliderPrimitive.Range className="absolute h-full bg-primary" />
    </SliderPrimitive.Track>
    <SliderPrimitive.Thumb className="block h-5 w-5 rounded-full border-2 border-primary bg-background ring-offset-background transition-colors focus-visible:outline-none focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-ring focus-visible:ring-offset-2 disabled:pointer-events-none disabled:opacity-50" />
  </SliderPrimitive.Root>
))
Slider.displayName = SliderPrimitive.Root.displayName

export { Slider }

client/src/components/ui/checkbox.tsx.content:
import * as React from "react"
import * as CheckboxPrimitive from "@radix-ui/react-checkbox"
import { Check } from "lucide-react"

import { cn } from "@/lib/utils"

const Checkbox = React.forwardRef<
  React.ElementRef<typeof CheckboxPrimitive.Root>,
  React.ComponentPropsWithoutRef<typeof CheckboxPrimitive.Root>
>(({ className, ...props }, ref) => (
  <CheckboxPrimitive.Root
    ref={ref}
    className={cn(
      "peer h-4 w-4 shrink-0 rounded-sm border border-primary ring-offset-background focus-visible:outline-none focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-ring focus-visible:ring-offset-2 disabled:cursor-not-allowed disabled:opacity-50 data-[state=checked]:bg-primary data-[state=checked]:text-primary-foreground",
      className
    )}
    {...props}
  >
    <CheckboxPrimitive.Indicator
      className={cn("flex items-center justify-center text-current")}
    >
      <Check className="h-4 w-4" />
    </CheckboxPrimitive.Indicator>
  </CheckboxPrimitive.Root>
))
Checkbox.displayName = CheckboxPrimitive.Root.displayName

export { Checkbox }

replit.md.content:
# Overview

This project is the "SEO Analyzer Pro," a single-page web application offering 8 comprehensive SEO analysis tools with a single URL input. Its core purpose is to provide a detailed SEO audit, Google Ads landing page experience analysis, AEO/AI SEO analysis, GEO/Generative Engine Optimization analysis, broken link checking, image optimization, internal linking analysis, and sitemap/robots.txt validation. All results are displayed on one scrollable page with collapsible sections.

The project features a 3-tier monetization model (Free, Basic, Pro) to convert users from lead magnets to paid subscriptions. It aims to deliver significant value through estimated savings and impact, technical fix guides, and advanced comparison features, positioning itself as a crucial tool for digital marketers and webmasters.

# User Preferences

Preferred communication style: Simple, everyday language.

# System Architecture

## Core Application Structure

The application is a single-page web tool built with a React/TypeScript frontend (Vite, Shadcn/ui, Tailwind CSS) and a Node.js/Express.js/TypeScript backend. It operates on a single route (`/`) and uses RESTful API endpoints for all analyses and PDF generation. Data storage is primarily in-memory for session management, with Drizzle ORM configured for future PostgreSQL integration.

## Monetization & Gating System

A 3-tier model (Free, Basic, Pro) is implemented with on-screen content gating and PDF download restrictions. Free users see blurred issues and require email capture for basic PDFs. Basic and Pro tiers unlock more content, recommendations, and technical fix guides. Payment processing is handled via Stripe, and email capture uses MailerLite. An in-memory session store manages user sessions and their paid tier status for 24 hours. Rate limiting restricts free analyses per IP.

### How the Session Works (No Cookies)
- The app uses a **server-side in-memory Map** (not cookies or localStorage) to track sessions
- When a user runs an analysis, the backend generates a UUID (`sessionId`) and stores the results in memory
- The `sessionId` is returned to the frontend and held in React state only — refreshing the page loses the session
- Each session entry tracks: analysis data, email (null until captured), `paidTier` ("free" | "basic" | "pro"), and a timestamp
- Sessions **auto-expire after 24 hours** — a cleanup job runs hourly and removes old entries
- If the server restarts, all sessions are lost (in-memory only)

### Payment Flow (Stripe)
1. User clicks "Basic Report" (€29) or "Pro Report" (€249) in the results dashboard
2. Frontend sends `POST /api/create-checkout` with `{ sessionId, tier }` to the backend
3. Backend creates a Stripe Checkout Session, embedding `sessionId` and `tier` in Stripe's `metadata`
4. User is redirected to Stripe's hosted payment page
5. After payment, Stripe redirects back to `/?payment=success&session={sessionId}&tier={tier}`
6. Stripe also sends a `checkout.session.completed` webhook to `POST /api/stripe-webhook`
7. The webhook calls `markPaid(sessionId, tier)` to upgrade the session's `paidTier` in the in-memory store
8. The frontend reads the URL params and updates its local `paidTier` state to unlock on-screen content

### What Free Users See On-Screen
- Overall score dashboard with individual tool scores (always visible)
- Value/savings estimate cards per section (always visible when issues exist)
- **Blurred tease**: Each section shows a `FreeKeyFindings` component — the top 3-5 issues with a visual blur effect (3 clear items, 4th fading with gradient overlay, 5th fully blurred)
- A `LockedOverlay` at the bottom of each section with "Unlock" upgrade buttons
- **PDF**: Requires email capture first (sent to MailerLite). Free PDF contains overall scores + top 3 critical issues only

### What Basic Subscribers (€29) See On-Screen
After paying, `paidTier` is set to `"basic"`. The gating logic is `paidTier === 'free' ? (blur tease) : (full content)`, so Basic unlocks all detailed data. Anything gated behind `paidTier === 'pro'` stays hidden.

| Section | What Basic Gets | What's Hidden (Pro Only) |
|---|---|---|
| **SEO Audit** | Full tabbed view — all checks grouped by category (Technical, Performance, Accessibility, Keywords, Content) + Compare tab | "Fix:" instructions under each failing check (code snippets) |
| **Google Ads** | Overview, Checks, Impact, and Compare tabs — all checks with pass/fail status | Recommendations tab; inline impact text; TechnicalFixBlock code examples |
| **AEO** | All checks, 5 category scores, Schema Generator, AI Search Preview, Content Gap Finder, Citation Score | Recommendations list |
| **GEO** | All checks, 5 category scores, Generative Readiness Score | Recommendations list |
| **Broken Links** | Full link table — all broken, redirected, and working links with status codes | Nothing hidden — Basic sees everything |
| **Image Optimization** | Optimization metrics (alt text, lazy loading, dimensions, srcset, modern format bars) + image issues list | Recommendations card with code snippet fix guides |
| **Internal Linking** | Links tab (full table with anchor text, nofollow, depth) + Anchor Distribution tab | Recommendations tab |
| **Sitemap/Robots.txt** | Robots.txt tab (directives, user agents, paths) + Sitemap tab (URL count, structure) | Recommendations tab with config templates |

**Basic PDF**: All checks and detailed data, but no fix instructions or recommendations.

### What Pro Subscribers (€249) See On-Screen
After paying, `paidTier` is set to `"pro"`. Every `paidTier === 'pro'` gate opens. Pro gets everything Basic sees, plus:

- **SEO Audit**: "Fix:" text under every failing/warning check — actionable code snippets (e.g., `<title>` tag examples, meta description templates, robots.txt directives)
- **Google Ads**: Recommendations tab with prioritized improvements; inline impact text under each check; TechnicalFixBlock components with code examples
- **AEO**: Full recommendations list with prioritized AI readiness actions
- **GEO**: Recommendations section with generative engine optimization suggestions
- **Image Optimization**: Recommendations card with HTML code examples (alt text, lazy loading, srcset, WebP conversion CLI commands)
- **Internal Linking**: Recommendations tab with anchor text, nofollow, and link depth suggestions
- **Sitemap/Robots.txt**: Recommendations tab with robots.txt templates, XML sitemap structure, lastmod format guidance

**Pro PDF**: Complete report with all recommendations, technical fix guides, and code snippets.

### Access Code System
- After payment (Stripe or dev mode), users receive a unique **access code** (e.g., `PRO-7X9K2M` or `BAS-A3F2D1`)
- Each code grants **5 URL scans** — each "Run All Tests" analysis uses 1 scan credit
- Compare features within an existing session are free (no scan deduction)
- Access codes are stored in `server/services/accessCodeStore.ts` (in-memory Map, no expiry until scans run out)
- Each code tracks: `code`, `tier`, `scansRemaining`, `scansTotal`, `email`, `sessionIds[]`, `stripeSessionId`
- Users enter their code in the "Have an access code?" field below the URL input
- After activation, a tier badge shows (e.g., "Pro · 4 scans left") with the code displayed
- Access codes survive page refreshes — users just re-enter the code to resume
- **API endpoints**: `POST /api/redeem-code` (validate & get info), `POST /api/get-access-code` (retrieve after payment)
- The `POST /api/master-analyze` endpoint accepts an optional `accessCode` field; when provided, it deducts a scan and skips rate limiting

### Access Duration & Limitations
- **On-screen access lasts for the browser session only** — the unlocked content stays visible as long as the user keeps the page open. Refreshing or closing the tab loses access because `paidTier` is held in React state (not persisted)
- **Access codes persist across sessions** — users can re-enter their access code after refreshing to regain paid tier access and use remaining scans
- **Backend session lasts 24 hours** — the server stores the analysis data and payment status in memory for 24 hours. After that, the session is automatically deleted
- **Access codes never expire** — they remain valid until all 5 scans are used (but are lost on server restart since they're in-memory)
- **PDFs are permanent** — once downloaded, the PDF file belongs to the user forever. This is the real deliverable. The on-screen access is a preview; the PDF is what they're paying for
- **No login or account system** — there are no user accounts. Access codes serve as lightweight credentials for returning users
- **Server restarts clear all data** — since sessions and access codes are in-memory only, a server restart loses everything. The PDF download should happen in the same session as payment

### Quick Reference: Basic vs Pro
- **Basic tells you *what's wrong*** — all raw data, all checks, all scores, all metrics, competitor comparisons
- **Pro tells you *how to fix it*** — all fix instructions, all recommendations, all technical fix guides with code examples

### Dev Mode Bypass
- An amber "Dev Preview:" dropdown appears in the dashboard when `NODE_ENV !== 'production'`
- Selecting a tier immediately updates the frontend `paidTier` state — no payment required
- The backend also skips payment validation in development mode for PDF downloads
- If Stripe is not configured and user is in dev mode, clicking "Get Basic/Pro Report" generates a real access code immediately without payment
- Hidden automatically in production builds

### Rate Limiting
- 3 free analyses per IP per 24-hour rolling window (server/services/rateLimiter.ts)
- Paid analyses are not counted against the limit

## Frontend Architecture

- **Framework**: React with TypeScript (Vite).
- **UI**: Shadcn/ui components (Radix UI) and Tailwind CSS.
- **State Management**: TanStack Query for server state, React Hook Form with Zod for forms.
- **Routing**: Wouter for lightweight client-side routing.

## Backend Architecture

- **Runtime**: Node.js with Express.js (TypeScript, ES modules).
- **Data Storage**: In-memory store (Map) for ephemeral analysis sessions, designed for PostgreSQL integration with Drizzle ORM.
- **API Design**: RESTful endpoints.
- **Utilities**: Cheerio for web scraping, PDFKit for PDF generation, Date-fns for date utilities.

## SEO Analysis Engine

Utilizes Cheerio for HTML parsing and the fetch API for content retrieval. It covers technical SEO, performance, accessibility, keyword analysis, and content quality, employing a weighted scoring system (0-100).

## Specialized Analyzers

- **Google Ads Landing Page Experience Analyzer**: Evaluates page performance for Google Ads, including TTFB, CDN detection, cache fragmentation, and redirect chains.
- **AEO (Answer Engine Optimization) Analyzer**: Assesses content for AI readiness across structured data, content format, authority, semantic structure, and AI accessibility. Includes features like schema markup generation, AI search preview, and competitor comparison.
- **GEO (Generative Engine Optimization) Analyzer**: Analyzes content for generative AI engines based on source authority, content fluency, unique value, entity optimization, and multi-format content. Offers a Generative Readiness Score and competitor comparison.

## Site Tools

Integrated tools include:
- **Broken Link Checker**: Identifies broken, redirected, and working links.
- **Image Optimization**: Checks alt text, lazy loading, dimensions, modern formats, and file sizes.
- **Internal Linking Analysis**: Evaluates anchor text quality, nofollow attributes, and link depth.
- **Sitemap & Robots.txt Validator**: Parses and validates sitemap and robots.txt directives.

# External Dependencies

## UI and Styling
- **Radix UI**: Accessible component primitives.
- **Tailwind CSS**: Utility-first CSS framework.
- **Lucide React**: Icon library.
- **Class Variance Authority**: Type-safe component variant management.

## Data and State Management
- **TanStack Query**: Server state synchronization and caching.
- **React Hook Form**: Form state management and validation.
- **Zod**: Runtime type validation and schema definition.
- **Drizzle ORM**: Type-safe database operations (configured for PostgreSQL).

## Server and Analysis
- **Express.js**: Web application framework.
- **Cheerio**: Server-side HTML parsing.
- **PDFKit**: PDF document generation.
- **Date-fns**: Date manipulation utilities.

## Integrations
- **Stripe**: Payment processing for subscriptions.
- **MailerLite**: Email marketing and subscriber management.

## Database
- **Neon Database**: Serverless PostgreSQL database service (planned integration).
- **Drizzle Kit**: Database migrations and schema management (planned use).

import * as React from "react" import * as NavigationMenuPrimitive from "@radix-ui/react-navigation-menu" import { cva } from "class-variance-authority" import { ChevronDown } from "lucide-react" import { cn } from "@/lib/utils" const NavigationMenu = React.forwardRef< React.ElementRef, React.ComponentPropsWithoutRef >(({ className, children, ...props }, ref) => ( {children} )) NavigationMenu.displayName = NavigationMenuPrimitive.Root.displayName const NavigationMenuList = React.forwardRef< React.ElementRef, React.ComponentPropsWithoutRef >(({ className, ...props }, ref) => ( )) NavigationMenuList.displayName = NavigationMenuPrimitive.List.displayName const NavigationMenuItem = NavigationMenuPrimitive.Item const navigationMenuTriggerStyle = cva( "group inline-flex h-10 w-max items-center justify-center rounded-md bg-background px-4 py-2 text-sm font-medium transition-colors hover:bg-accent hover:text-accent-foreground focus:bg-accent focus:text-accent-foreground focus:outline-none disabled:pointer-events-none disabled:opacity-50 data-[state=open]:text-accent-foreground data-[state=open]:bg-accent/50 data-[state=open]:hover:bg-accent data-[state=open]:focus:bg-accent" ) const NavigationMenuTrigger = React.forwardRef< React.ElementRef, React.ComponentPropsWithoutRef >(({ className, children, ...props }, ref) => ( {children}{" "} )) NavigationMenuTrigger.displayName = NavigationMenuPrimitive.Trigger.displayName const NavigationMenuContent = React.forwardRef< React.ElementRef, React.ComponentPropsWithoutRef >(({ className, ...props }, ref) => ( )) NavigationMenuContent.displayName = NavigationMenuPrimitive.Content.displayName const NavigationMenuLink = NavigationMenuPrimitive.Link const NavigationMenuViewport = React.forwardRef< React.ElementRef, React.ComponentPropsWithoutRef >(({ className, ...props }, ref) => (
)) NavigationMenuViewport.displayName = NavigationMenuPrimitive.Viewport.displayName const NavigationMenuIndicator = React.forwardRef< React.ElementRef, React.ComponentPropsWithoutRef >(({ className, ...props }, ref) => (
)) NavigationMenuIndicator.displayName = NavigationMenuPrimitive.Indicator.displayName export { navigationMenuTriggerStyle, NavigationMenu, NavigationMenuList, NavigationMenuItem, NavigationMenuContent, NavigationMenuTrigger, NavigationMenuLink, NavigationMenuIndicator, NavigationMenuViewport, }

client/src/components/ui/scroll-area.tsx.content:
import * as React from "react"
import * as ScrollAreaPrimitive from "@radix-ui/react-scroll-area"

import { cn } from "@/lib/utils"

const ScrollArea = React.forwardRef<
  React.ElementRef<typeof ScrollAreaPrimitive.Root>,
  React.ComponentPropsWithoutRef<typeof ScrollAreaPrimitive.Root>
>(({ className, children, ...props }, ref) => (
  <ScrollAreaPrimitive.Root
    ref={ref}
    className={cn("relative overflow-hidden", className)}
    {...props}
  >
    <ScrollAreaPrimitive.Viewport className="h-full w-full rounded-[inherit]">
      {children}
    </ScrollAreaPrimitive.Viewport>
    <ScrollBar />
    <ScrollAreaPrimitive.Corner />
  </ScrollAreaPrimitive.Root>
))
ScrollArea.displayName = ScrollAreaPrimitive.Root.displayName

const ScrollBar = React.forwardRef<
  React.ElementRef<typeof ScrollAreaPrimitive.ScrollAreaScrollbar>,
  React.ComponentPropsWithoutRef<typeof ScrollAreaPrimitive.ScrollAreaScrollbar>
>(({ className, orientation = "vertical", ...props }, ref) => (
  <ScrollAreaPrimitive.ScrollAreaScrollbar
    ref={ref}
    orientation={orientation}
    className={cn(
      "flex touch-none select-none transition-colors",
      orientation === "vertical" &&
        "h-full w-2.5 border-l border-l-transparent p-[1px]",
      orientation === "horizontal" &&
        "h-2.5 flex-col border-t border-t-transparent p-[1px]",
      className
    )}
    {...props}
  >
    <ScrollAreaPrimitive.ScrollAreaThumb className="relative flex-1 rounded-full bg-border" />
  </ScrollAreaPrimitive.ScrollAreaScrollbar>
))
ScrollBar.displayName = ScrollAreaPrimitive.ScrollAreaScrollbar.displayName

export { ScrollArea, ScrollBar }
